A '''battle''' at Kothlis occurred after secret [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] construction data was secured during a [[Raid on the Suprosa|raid]] on the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Suprosa]]''. [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces took a [[Computer/Legends|computer]] core holding the [[Death Star II plans]] from the freighter and delivered it to a [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]] safehouse on the colony [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]]. The site was raided by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]], and although rebel [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] was captured, the data core was smuggled out. The core was transferred to the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Razor (CR90 corvette)|''Razor'']]. A battle started over the planet when the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Motivator (Imperial-class)|''Motivator'']] arrived and captured the ''Razor''. Rebel reinforcements arrived and destroyed the Star Destroyer, which plunged to the planet with the corvette in its hangar bay. A commando raid was launched to recover the data, and although surviving Imperial forces deployed to defend the wreckage, [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine's]] [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] took the data before the reactor went critical.

==Prelude==
[[File:Battle_of_Bothawui.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Rebels engaging the ''Suprosa''.]]
The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] was in a desperate struggle after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|loss]] of its [[Echo Base/Legends|base]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> and rumors began reaching [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] about the construction of a larger, more power [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]].<ref name="EGW">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> The [[Bothan Spynet/Legends|Bothan Spynet]] received a tip from the [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] criminal organization that the [[Death Star II plans]] were being secretly transported on a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Suprosa]]''. The Alliance had also successfully destroyed the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Vengeance (Executor-class)|Vengeance]] during the [[Battle of Nocto]] although they suffered heavy casualties including several [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-Wing]] starfighters. [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], along with the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Dash Rendar/Legends|Dash Rendar]], traveled to [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] and met their contact [[Koth Melan]]<ref name="SOTE" /> in 3.5 ABY<ref name="NEC" /> to train the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan's]] [[Blue Squadron (Bothan)|Blue Squadron]] to [[Raid on the Suprosa|intercept]] the freighter.<ref name="SOTE" /> There was a vacancy, and Rebel pilot [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] was assigned to the raid.<ref name="XWA" /> The Rebels intercepted the freighter, which was heavily armed, and lost six ships in the raid. Skywalker disabled the freighter,<ref name="SOTE" /> and Rendar boarded and took the data core containing the plans.<ref name="SOTE vg">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (video game)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' video game]]</ref> The plans were then brought to the Bothan colony [[Kothlis/Legends|Kothlis]].<ref name="SOTE" />

==The battle==
===Raid on the safehouse===
{{Quote|Hey hey hey! Look at this, boys! Scan sector Tarp-Hard-Xenon.|A Bothan technician|<ref name="SOTE" />}}
[[File:Luke-sote5.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Luke Skywalker defending against Barabel attackers.]]
Bothan technicians began decoding the data in a Bothan Spynet safehouse down on the planet,<ref name="SOTE" /> but the Imperials tracked the computer to Kothlis.<ref name="XWA" /> After a Bothan [[Technician/Legends|technician]] was close to decoding the computer core, the safehouse's door exploded. The building was attacked by a team of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] looking for the bounty on Skywalker. As [[Lightfight|fire]] was traded in the storehouse, Melan fell to [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire while Skywalker used his [[Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to defend himself. A Bothan grabbed the computer and ran off, and Luke was captured by the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Skahtul]].<ref name="SOTE" /> The technicians managed to take the data core away.<ref name="NEC" />

===Battle above Kothlis===
{{Quote|Wedge here. We can't let that cruiser get away. Attack the shield generators. Once the shields are down, we can attack the command deck. X- and A-wing groups, keep those fighters off our back.|Rogue Leader|<ref name="Leader" />}}
The data managed to reach the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] [[Razor (CR90 corvette)|''Razor'']] in orbit around Kothlis. As the freighter proceeded to rendezvous with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Motivator (Imperial-class)|''Motivator'']] jumped out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and deployed its contingent of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] and [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|interceptors]], and managed to secure the ''Razor'' in a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]].<ref name="Leader" />

At that time, rebel reinforcements which were supposed to rendezvous with the corvette<ref name="RS II guide">''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]''</ref> decanted from hyperspace. [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], flying three [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing starfighters]], four [[T-65B X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65B X-wing starfighters]], and three [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|RZ-1 A-wing interceptors]], escorted the medical [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|frigate]] [[Redemption/Legends|''Redemption'']]. The rebels noticed the Star Destroyer, and [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] ordered his [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmen]] into battle, telling them to concentrate on the Destroyer's shield generators. He also told the X-wing and A-wing groups to attack the TIE fighters.<ref name="Leader" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader}}
During the battle, Rogue Squadron spotted a [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttle]] preparing to dock with the ''Motivator''. Wedge Antilles then took out the shuttle, and proceeded to recover a supply of advanced proton bombs.<ref name="Leader" />

The rogues skirmished with the TIE starfighters, and Antilles destroyed the [[Octuple barbette turbolaser|ion cannons]] facing the ''Redemption.'' The [[Redemption captain|captain]] reported that the frigate was under attack, and Antilles commed the Rogues, ordering them to protect the frigate at all costs. He was also able to cause additional damage to the Destroyer. Pressing the attack, he took out the bridge deflector shields and the reactor. The ''Motivator'' then started to move into a position to jump to hyperspace with the ''Razor'' still in its hangar bay, but Antilles approached on a vector and took out the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|command deck]].<ref name="Leader" />
{{Endgame}}

The Star Destroyer then started a slow plummet to the surface as Rogue Squadron strafed the ship. Antilles suggested that they contact Alliance command for support, specializing that they had to go down to the surface of the planet.<ref name="Leader" />

[[File:Razor.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The ''Motivator'' capturing the ''Razor'']]

===Battle on Kothlis===
{{Quote|Another hit like that and we've got it...Hull penetrated.|Wedge Antilles using a Y-wing to bomb the wreckage of the ''Motivator''|<ref name="Leader" />}}
[[File:ClashOnKothlis-SWV.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Imperial forces battle near the grounded Star Destroyer.]]
As the Star Destroyer crashed into the surface, the surviving crew set up defenses. An Alliance [[Unidentified GR-75 medium transport (Kothlis)|GR-75]] [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|transport]] carrying [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] and a company of [[Commando/Legends|commandos]] subsequently arrived to secure ''Razor''{{'s}} computer core. As three of Rogue Squadron's X-wing starfighters escorted the transport to the crash site, Madine contacted them to provide support.<ref name="Leader" /> The transport was under fire from several TIE [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]].<ref name="RS II guide" /> The rebels were surprised to find a portion of the Star Destroyer's ground forces had survived the crash. Madine, after discovering that the ''Motivator's'' core was increasing in temperature, and was most likely going to go critical within minutes, was forced to land the transport on the nearby beach as he was unwilling to risk trying to get close to the doomed Star Destroyer.<ref name="Leader" />

Still-operational [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]] and [[All Terrain Personal Transport/Legends|AT-PTs]] blasted their way through the Star Destroyer's hull and began to attack the rebel transport. The transport deployed three [[T-47 airspeeder/Legends|T-47 airspeeders]], and Rogue Squadron landed and switched craft. They then used their [[Power harpoon launcher|harpoon and tow cables]] to trip up the AT-ATs,<ref name="Leader" /> concentrating on the foremost walker, which was in the shallowest water.<ref name="RS II guide" />

{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader}}
During the battle, Wedge Antilles noticed an object within a small hole of what remained of the ''Motivator''{{'s}} command bridge. He briefly broke away from the battle and ended up picking up a supply of homing proton torpedoes.<ref name="Leader" />

While Rogue Squadron provided covering fire and targeted the armored vehicles, Madine and his commandos engaged in a firefight with [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] and their [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-web blaster]] nests. Antilles and the rogues then switched to four [[BTL-A4 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL-A4 Y-wing starfighters]] at the transport and started bombing the AT-PTs which were approaching under the surf.<ref name="Leader" />

After neutralizing any remaining functional batteries and destroying the Imperial armor, Rogue Squadron covered Madine's charge to the Star Destroyer. Madine informed Antilles when the last of the walkers were destroyed. The ground troops took orders from the Rogues during their attack. Eventually, Madine reported they had spotted weak spots in the Star Destroyer's hull, and Rogue Squadron then proceeded to bomb the weak point to grant him and his men entry to the Star Destroyer.<ref name="Leader" />
{{Endgame}}

Madine and the commandos succeeded in retrieving the data from ''Razor'', and the transport took off and circled around, and the surviving commandos boarded the transport as it landed. The rebels fled Kothlis while Rogue Squadron engaged pursuing TIE fighters.<ref name="Leader" /> The information—the Death Star II plans—was later delivered to Alliance leader [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Many Bothans died to bring us this information.|Mon Mothma briefing the Alliance Fleet prior to the Battle of Endor|<ref name="SWE6">{{Film|VI}}</ref>}}
The information collected from the data core, coupled with other information collected by Rebel agent [[Tay Vanis]], convinced the Alliance High Command of the reality of the new Imperial battlestation.<ref name="EGW" /> Alliance High Command ordered the fleet to disperse to discover more information.<ref name="XWA" /> Skywalker was later able to escape the bounty hunters by using his [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]],<ref name="SOTE" /> and joined up with [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]].<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> The Alliance later launched an [[Battle of Endor/Legends|attack]] on the under-construction battlestation. Although the capture of the plans was part of a trap laid by [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="SOTE" /> the battle resulted in his death, the redemption of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], and the destruction of the superweapon.<ref name="SWE6" />

==Behind the scenes==
The battle of Kothlis first appeared in [[1995]] in [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars'': ''TIE Fighter'' Collector's CD-ROM]] during the mission titled 'Strike on Kothlis' at which point the Empire captures the [[Kothlis II]], a space station in orbit around Kothlis. The next appearance was in [[1996]] with the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire|''Shadows of the Empire'']] multimedia project. A quote from that chapter of the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|novel]] includes a reference to [[1138 (easter egg)|''THX-1138'']]. There are two conflicting sources for the capture of the ''Razor.'' The battle as described above is the storyline as it appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' in two consecutive missions: ''Razor Rendezvous'' and ''Vengeance on Kothlis''. In the [[1999]] video game ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' the story goes a little different, though the outcome is the same. This article follows the ''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader'' version, as it is the later source, and was pictured in the art book ''[[Star Wars Art: Visions]]''.

The surviving Bothan spies flee from Kothlis on ''[[Fey'lya's Pride]]'', with the stolen Imperial computer to ''[[Kothlis II]]''. Around that time, Darth Vader's [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]] has arrived and begun it's attack on ''Kothlis II'', where the computer is transferred on to the [[Razor (CR90 corvette)|''Razor'']]. Under covering fire from [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']] the Razor makes it safely to hyperspace.

While en route to its rendezvous with the [[Independence/Legends|''Independence'']], the Razor, has come under attack by an Imperial pursuit group. The corvette is eventually captured by the [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']], but managed to launch an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] which is picked up by the civilian [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Dreadnaught]] [[Mercury (Dreadnaught-class)|''Mercury'']]. ''Mercury'' escaped to hyperspace to rendezvous with the ''Independence''. While transferring the escape pod to the ''Independence'' the ''Avenger'' and the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] emerge from hyperspace and begin their attack. The ''Mercury'' and ''Independence'' manage to escape once more and thus finally securing the Death Star II plans in the hands of the Alliance.

In ''Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader'', any damage performed to the Star Destroyer in the first mission carries over to the next mission. This article assumes the player took out the ion cannons to protect the ''Redemption''. At the end of the mission, the player can deliver the coup-de-grace on the ''Motivator'' by performing a kamikaze run on the ship, the requirements of the mission stated the player can still get a Gold Medal if they only lose one life, and the ''Rebel Strike'' Official Nintendo Strategy Guide suggests that the player does so to finish the mission with a Gold Medal. This article assumes the player did not do so, as Antilles [[canon]]ically survives, and respawns are a game mechanic. If the player destroys the Star Destroyer before the ''Razor'' goes into the hangar bay, it can be seen floating in space in the ending cutscene, although it canonically falls to the surface with the ''Motivator.''

In the second mission, the player must protect the commandos. They can have casualties but not too many, although the mission will fail if the transport gets destroyed. The default craft for the first mission is the B-wing, and the default craft for the start of the second mission is the X-wing. There are two separate tech upgrades to be found in the missions. Finding them fulfills the bonus objectives.
